components.title: Patch Management Policy
components.description: Defines the organization's requirements for identifying, testing, approving, and deploying security patches and updates across all systems including operating systems, applications, firmware, and network devices. Establishes severity-based deployment timeframes and mandatory vulnerability scanning to verify patch compliance.
components.purpose: Ensure timely remediation of vulnerabilities through structured patch management.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Patch Management Policy.

implemented-requirements.description: This policy establishes flaw remediation requirements aligned to SI-2: critical/high CVSSv3 patches applied within 30 days of release; medium severity within 90 days; low severity within 180 days. Emergency patching procedures are defined for actively exploited vulnerabilities (patch within 14 days or mitigate).

This policy mandates vulnerability scanning: authenticated scans of all systems at least monthly; web application scans quarterly; results reviewed by the security team within 5 business days and remediation tracked in the POA&M.

This policy requires that security patches are tested against a representative configuration baseline before production deployment. Emergency exceptions require CISO approval and a post-deployment validation plan.
This policy requires maintenance of an accurate system component inventory as a prerequisite for patch management. The CMDB must be updated within 5 business days of any hardware or software change.
This policy requires that anti-malware software definitions are updated within 24 hours of vendor release. Systems without current definitions are quarantined until remediated.
